careerWorlds.title: Health, Care & Clinical Practice

careerWorlds.title: Society, Culture, Languages & Global Affairs
careerWorlds.description: For people interested in culture, language, identity, global issues, and understanding how societies communicate and change.

careerWorlds.title: Architecture, Built Environment & Spatial Design
careerWorlds.description: For people drawn to shaping spaces, buildings, and places through a blend of design imagination, long-term thinking, and practical structure.

careerWorlds.title: Psychology, Behaviour & Human Insight
careerWorlds.description: For people interested in understanding how people think, feel, behave, and change across individual, group, or societal contexts.

subjects.title: Education
subjects.signatureRationale: Matches your existing subject bank logic. Connector leads because the subject is people-focused, Visionary reflects shaping growth and futures, and Organizer supports planning and dependable delivery.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Counselling, psychotherapy, and occupational therapy
subjects.signatureRationale: This guide sits between health and psychology. Connector leads for direct support and relational work, Thinker reflects evidence-informed practice, and Organizer stays important because intervention work requires structure and professional discipline.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Architecture and planning
subjects.signatureRationale: Matches the architecture signature already present in your subject bank. Creator leads for spatial design, Visionary stays second for long-term placemaking and future thinking, and Organizer reflects the technical and regulatory discipline the field requires.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: English language
subjects.signatureRationale: English language focuses strongly on communication and how language works. Connector leads for social communication, Thinker supports analysis, and Creator reflects expressive and interpretive language use.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Sociology
subjects.signatureRationale: Builds from your existing Sociology seed by adding Thinker in third place. Sociology is social and systems-oriented first, but still analytical in how it studies institutions, inequality, and change.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Art
subjects.signatureRationale: This subject follows the creative world closely: expressive making first, imaginative originality second, and exploratory experimentation third.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Digital media, production, and technology
subjects.signatureRationale: This subject clearly belongs to media/communication, but it is more production- and tool-led than pure brand communication. Creator leads, Thinker supports technical workflow, and Explorer reflects experimentation with formats and platforms.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Computer science
subjects.signatureRationale: Matches your existing subject bank exactly. Thinker leads for deep problem-solving, Creator reflects building digital products and systems, and Explorer captures experimentation with new technologies.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: History
subjects.signatureRationale: History is analytical first, but it also depends on constructing interpretations and understanding long-term change. Creator reflects narrative/interpretive synthesis, while Visionary captures big-picture historical perspective.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Geography
subjects.signatureRationale: Geography sits at the boundary of environment and society. Explorer leads for place-based inquiry, Thinker supports systems analysis, and Visionary reflects climate, development, and long-term global issues.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Accounting and finance

subjects.title: Biology
subjects.signatureRationale: Biology is fundamentally investigative, so Thinker leads. Explorer stays high because discovery and field/lab curiosity matter, while Organizer reflects scientific method and careful evidence handling.
subjects.evidenceNote: Evidence subdimensions are selected from the uploaded CareerDNA subdimension-to-archetype matrix and then refined where the subject has a clear domain-specific psychological profile.

subjects.title: Building and construction

subjects.title: Archaeology
subjects.signatureRationale: Archaeology sits in the society/culture world but with a more investigative and methodical profile. Thinker leads for evidence-based interpretation, Explorer reflects discovery, and Organizer supports careful reconstruction and method.
